title: Wi-Fi Troubleshooting: Can't Connect to Corporate Network
text:
Q: What should I do if I can't connect to the corporate Wi-Fi network?
A: If you're having trouble connecting to the corporate Wi-Fi network, follow these steps:

Step 1: Ensure Wi-Fi is enabled on your device. 

Windows: Click on the Wi-Fi icon in the taskbar and make sure it's turned on.

macOS: Click on the Wi-Fi icon in the menu bar and ensure 'Turn Wi-Fi On' is selected.

Step 2: Check if you are within range of the corporate Wi-Fi network. Move closer to the router if necessary.

Step 3: Verify that you are trying to connect to the correct network. Look for the network name (SSID) provided by the IT department.

Step 4: If prompted, enter the correct Wi-Fi password. Ensure there are no typos, as passwords are case-sensitive.

Step 5: Restart your device. Sometimes a simple reboot can resolve connectivity issues.

Step 6: Forget the network and reconnect. 

Windows: Go to Settings > Network & Internet > Wi-Fi > Manage known networks, select the network, and click 'Forget'.

macOS: Go to System Preferences > Network > Wi-Fi > Advanced, select the network, and click the '-' button to remove it.

Step 7: Reconnect to the network by selecting it from the available networks list and entering the password again.

Step 8: If you still cannot connect, try resetting your network settings. 

Windows: Go to Settings > Network & Internet > Status > Network reset.

macOS: Go to System Preferences > Network, select Wi-Fi, and click 'Advanced', then 'Renew DHCP Lease'.

If the issue persists after these steps, please contact the IT helpdesk for further assistance.
